articles.title: Iran Deal Hopes Boost Emerging Market Assets as Oil Slides
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose Monday as signs that the US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices lower and boosted risk appetite.

articles.title: Oil Slides as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Hormuz
articles.summary: West Texas Intermediate crude briefly fell below $90 a barrel for the first time in almost three weeks amid signs the US and Iran were progressing toward an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Gold Gains as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
articles.summary: Gold advanced after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows, easing inflation fears.

articles.title: Hungary Sees May 28 Deal With EU on Frozen Funds, PM Magyar Says
articles.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he’ll sign a political accord with the head of the European Union’s executive on May 28 on the release of the bloc’s frozen funds.

articles.title: Cox Group Took 20% Bridge Loan for Iberdrola Mexico Deal
articles.summary: Spanish utility Cox ABG Group SA took a bridge loan to help fund a $4.2 billion asset acquisition in Mexico and placed shares as collateral.

articles.title: Vigilant ECB Hasn’t Yet Seen Second-Round Effects, Villeroy Says
articles.summary: A spike in energy costs hasn’t yet turned into second-round effects in other parts of the euro-area economy, according to outgoing European Central Bank Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de G

articles.title: Latest ‘Star Wars’ Film ‘Mandalorian’ Debuts at $100 Million
articles.summary: The Mandalorian and Grogu, the first film in the Star Wars franchise in seven years, was the highest-grossing movie in the US and Canada over the four-day holiday weekend with $100 million in ticket s

articles.title: Ghana to Start Buying 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Ouput From June
articles.summary: Ghana’s central bank said it plans to increase gold purchases from the West African nation’s large-scale producers to 30% of their output from 20%, starting June 1.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Gain as Crude Oil Drops on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened.

articles.title: European Stocks Advance on Iran Deal Hopes; Delivery Hero Jumps
articles.summary: European shares advanced as oil declined, with the US and Iran inching toward a deal to end the nearly-three-month war. Italian stocks notched a fresh record.
